perturbation.original_text: 4.2.1 You shall pay to us a royalty fee based on revenue generated by Unit Franchisees (the "Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee") equal to twenty-five percent (25%) of aggregate royalty fees paid to you by Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory pursuant to their Unit Franchise Agreements. The Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee shall be paid by you to us in the manner provided in Section 4.3 of this Agreement by the fifteenth(15 th ) day of each calendar month based on royalty fees generated and received during the previous calendar month. 4.2.2 You shall pay to us a franchise sales royalty fee (the "Franchise Sales Royalty Fee") for each Unit Franchise you sell in the Master Territory as follows: twenty-five percent (25%) of the initial franchise fee collected from each Unit Franchisee upon execution of the Unit Franchisee's Franchise Agreement (a "Unit Franchise Agreement"); provided, however, that if you elect to discount or reduce an initial franchise fee for any reason, the Franchise Sales Royalty Fee shall be payable to us as if the full initial franchise fee had been paid. The Franchise Sales Royalty Fee shall be paid by you to us at the same time and in the same manner as the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee provided in Section 4.2.1 above. Any Royalty Fee you collect from the Franchisee shall be immediately paid to Us, but no later than the fifteen (15 th ) day of each calendar month. A Unit Franchise shall be deemed to be sold to a Unit Franchisee on the date that you and the Unit Franchisee execute the Unit Franchise Agreement, irrespective of when the Unit Franchise begins operation. Fees and Royalties cannot be increased or decreased without our prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2.1 You shall pay to us a royalty fee based on revenue generated by Unit Franchisees (the "Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee") equal to twenty-five percent (25%) of aggregate royalty fees paid to you by Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory pursuant to their Unit Franchise Agreements. The Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee shall be paid by you to us in the manner provided in Section 4.3 of this Agreement by the fifteenth(15 th ) day of each calendar month based on royalty fees generated and received during the previous calendar month. 4.2.2 You shall pay to us a franchise sales royalty fee (the "Franchise Sales Royalty Fee") for each Unit Franchise you sell in the Master Territory as follows: twenty-five percent (25%) of the initial franchise fee collected from each Unit Franchisee upon execution of the Unit Franchisee's Franchise Agreement (a "Unit Franchise Agreement"); provided, however, that if you elect to discount or reduce an initial franchise fee for any reason, the Franchise Sales Royalty Fee shall be payable to us as if the full initial franchise fee had been paid. The Franchise Sales Royalty Fee shall be paid by you to us at the same time and in the same manner as the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee provided in Section 4.2.1 above. Any Royalty Fee you collect from the Franchisee shall be immediately paid to Us, but no later than the tenth (10 th ) day of each calendar month. A Unit Franchise shall be deemed to be sold to a Unit Franchisee on the date that you and the Unit Franchisee execute the Unit Franchise Agreement, irrespective of when the Unit Franchise begins operation. Fees and Royalties cannot be increased or decreased without our prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by establishing two different payment deadlines in the same section. Paragraph 4.2.1 says the payment should be made no later than the fifteenth, but paragraph 4.2.2 says the tenth, creating ambiguity, since it is referencing to the same type of fee. This contradiction makes it unclear which deadline is to be followed, and also since 4.2.2 referencing to 4.2.1, it doesn't provide a specific date, making the enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2.2 You shall pay to us a franchise sales royalty fee (the "Franchise Sales Royalty Fee") for each Unit Franchise you sell in the Master Territory as follows: twenty-five percent (25%) of the initial franchise fee collected from each Unit Franchisee upon execution of the Unit Franchisee's Franchise Agreement (a "Unit Franchise Agreement"); provided, however, that if you elect to discount or reduce an initial franchise fee for any reason, the Franchise Sales Royalty Fee shall be payable to us as if the full initial franchise fee had been paid. The Franchise Sales Royalty Fee shall be paid by you to us at the same time and in the same manner as the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee provided in Section 4.2.1 above. Any Royalty Fee you collect from the Franchisee shall be immediately paid to Us, but no later than the tenth (10 th ) day of each calendar month. A Unit Franchise shall be deemed to be sold to a Unit Franchisee on the date that you and the Unit Franchisee execute the Unit Franchise Agreement, irrespective of when the Unit Franchise begins operation. Fees and Royalties cannot be increased or decreased without our prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.2.2 introduces a different payment deadline (10th of the month) compared to the original text's reference to section 4.2.1 (15th of the month). This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the payment deadline, making it unclear which date should be followed, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text is located within the changed section, as the modification introduces two conflicting timelines.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5.1 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: broad form comprehensive general liability coverage against claims for employment practices coverage, bodily and personal injury, death and property damage caused by or occurring in conjunction with the conduct of business by you pursuant to this Agreement and broad form contractual liability coverage, including errors and omissions coverage, under one or more policies of insurance containing minimum liability coverage prescribed by us from time to time, but in no event in an amount less than Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) aggregate. Such insurance shall not have a deductible or self-insured retention in excess of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000);
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5.1 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: broad form comprehensive general liability coverage against claims for employment practices coverage, bodily and personal injury, death and property damage caused by or occurring in conjunction with the conduct of business by you pursuant to this Agreement and broad form contractual liability coverage, including errors and omissions coverage, under one or more policies of insurance containing minimum liability coverage prescribed by us from time to time, but in no event in an amount less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) aggregate. Such insurance shall not have a deductible or self-insured retention in excess of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000);
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the general liability insurance coverage amount required. The amount here in section 5.5.1 is changed from Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) to One Million Dollars ($1,000,000). This contradicts section 5.5.2, causing ambiguity. This could lead to disputes about the level of coverage required and the master franchisee's compliance with the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5.2 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: automobile liability insurance coverage, including owned and non-owned vehicles, with limits of not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) per occurrence;
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.5.1 reduces the minimum general liability coverage from Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) to One Million Dollars ($1,000,000). Section 5.5.2 specifies automobile liability insurance coverage with limits of not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) per occurrence. While both sections now specify the same amount, it creates an in-text contradiction. If the franchisee only obtains one million dollars in coverage as stated in section 5.5.1, it would not cover automobile liability as required in section 5.5.2. Therefore, the changed text in 5.5.1 creates an inconsistency and contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2.14 If, within ten (10) days after receipt of written notice from us that any required payment is overdue, you do not make such payment to us, our affiliates, or to your suppliers or creditors unless, with respect to your suppliers or creditors, you notify us of the existence on a bona fide dispute and takes immediate action to resolve it.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2.14 If, within ten (10) days after receipt of written notice from us that any required payment is overdue, you do not make such payment to us, our affiliates, or to your suppliers or creditors unless, with respect to your suppliers or creditors, you notify us of the existence on a bona fide dispute and takes immediate action to resolve it. However, if the payment due is more than $10,000, you will have fifteen (15) days after receipt of written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by establishing two different cure periods for overdue payments in termination defaults. While the section provides a 10-day cure period, and then the newly added text, increases to 15 days if the payment is above $10,000, this creates ambiguity and uncertainty, which contradicts the initial 10-day requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.3 Termination with Notice and Opportunity to Cure Except as otherwise provided in Sections 10.1 and 10.2 of this Agreement, you shall have thirty (30) days after your receipt from us of a written notice of default within which to remedy any default under this Agreement and to provide evidence thereof to us. If any such default is not cured within the specified time, or such longer period as applicable law may require, we shall have the right to terminate this Agreement by providing written notice of termination to you. You shall be in default pursuant to this Section 10.3 for failure to substantially comply with any of the requirements imposed by this Agreement, as it may from time to time reasonably be modified or supplemented by the Manuals, or your failure to carry out the terms of this Agreement in good faith.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 10.2.14 introduces a new cure period (15 days) for payments exceeding $10,000, which directly contradicts the original 10-day cure period stated in the same section. This creates an inconsistency within the same clause. Furthermore, section 10.3 provides a general 30-day cure period for defaults, and the addition in 10.2.14 creates an exception for payments and introduces different cure periods depending on the amount. While 10.3 says 'Except as otherwise provided in Sections 10.1 and 10.2,' the added text in 10.2.14 leads to in-text contradictions because it creates uncertainty about which cure period applies based on the payment amount. The inconsistency between the established 10-day and now 15-day cure period within 10.2.14 and the 30-day period in 10.3 creates an inconsistency as defined.
